configSchema.title: MCP Servers
configSchema.description: Register external Model Context Protocol servers so every Cinatra agent and package can call their tools. Admins can register globally; regular users can register personal servers visible only to them.

fields.label: API key storage
fields.whenReady: The connection service is configured. Add server does not yet persist an API key — register only servers that need no authentication.
fields.whenNotReady: The connection service is not configured. Add server does not yet persist an API key — register only servers that need no authentication.

fields.label: Registered servers
fields.emptyState: No external MCP servers registered yet.
fields.description: Admins see every registered server across every scope and owner; non-admins see only their own personal servers. Deleting a server is host-authorized.

fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Must be a public URL — LLM providers cannot reach localhost or private IPs.

fields.label: API key (optional)
fields.description: Leave blank if no authentication is required.

fields.label: Scope
fields.description: Only admins can create a global server.

fields.label: MCP Servers setup
fields.whenReady: The connection service is configured. To register a server: on the Setup tab, enter a Label and a public Server URL (LLM providers cannot reach localhost or private IPs — a private URL is flagged and never injected), choose a Scope, then click Add server. Registering a Global server (visible to every user and agent) requires platform admin; any authenticated user can register a Personal server. Note: Add server does not currently persist an API key, so register only servers that need no authentication. The Registered servers list on the Setup tab shows scope, private-URL, disabled, and API-key-configured badges, with a per-row delete action — admins see every registered server, non-admins see only their own personal servers.
fields.whenNotReady: The connection service is not configured. To register a server: on the Setup tab, enter a Label and a public Server URL (LLM providers cannot reach localhost or private IPs — a private URL is flagged and never injected), choose a Scope, then click Add server. Registering a Global server (visible to every user and agent) requires platform admin; any authenticated user can register a Personal server. Note: Add server does not currently persist an API key, so register only servers that need no authentication. The Registered servers list on the Setup tab shows scope, private-URL, disabled, and API-key-configured badges, with a per-row delete action — admins see every registered server, non-admins see only their own personal servers.
fields.description: Read-only setup guidance — nothing on this tab is saved.
